title: Cynanchum wilfordii
intro: Cynanchum wilfordii, also known as wilford's swallowwort, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum wilfordii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Cynanchum wilfordii include extracts, decoctions, and infusions, as well as standardized capsules and tablets, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Cynanchum wilfordii is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family consists of over 5,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Cynanchum is a large and diverse group within the Apocynaceae family, containing over 250 species of plants. Cynanchum wilfordii is a specific species within this genus, characterized by its unique characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Cynanchum wilfordii provides valuable information for botanists and researchers studying the plant's evolution, ecology, and potential uses.
uses: Cynanchum wilfordii, also known as Wu-Gombi in Korean, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various health conditions. The plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal uses also extend to treating gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Cynanchum wilfordii is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.

constituents: Cynanchum wilfordii, also known as the Insulin Plant, contains several active constituents, including glycosides and iridoid glycosides. The plant's iridoid glycosides have been shown to have potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. parts: Cynanchum wilfordii, also known as Baekjinae or Hwangma, is a plant used in traditional medicine in East Asia. The medicinal parts of Cynanchum wilfordii include the roots, stems, and leaves.
